metadata.logic_style: finite-trace LTL AST over static transition snapshots

description: Each trace element is a static transition snapshot, not a pure world state.
1. facts about the state immediately before the executed primitive action
2. the identity of the last executed primitive action
3. facts about the state immediately after that action
reason: This keeps atomic propositions static and symbolic.
It also lets preconditions and effects be stated without using request-style APs.

notes: The AP names are intentionally simple and compositional.
Geometry-dependent concepts are abstracted into derived APs that a Python verifier should compute.
The primitive actions match the simulator/environment API exactly: move_grasper, lower_grasper, raise_grasper, close_grasper, open_grasper, highlight_object, unhighlight_object.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_move_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was move_grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_lower_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was lower_grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_raise_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was raise_grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_close_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was close_grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_open_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was open_grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_highlight_object
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was highlight_object.

atomic_propositions.name: last_action_is_unhighlight_object
atomic_propositions.description: The last executed primitive action was unhighlight_object.

atomic_propositions.name: explicit_grasper_id_supplied
atomic_propositions.description: This transition uses an explicit grasper_id argument rather than relying on the default grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_grasper_exists
atomic_propositions.description: Before the action, a valid active grasper exists.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_object_exists
atomic_propositions.description: Before the action, the referenced object id exists.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_explicit_grasper_ref_is_valid_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: Before the action, any explicit grasper id refers to an object of kind=grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_grasper_lowered
atomic_propositions.description: Before the action, the active grasper is lowered.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_grasper_closed
atomic_propositions.description: Before the action, the active grasper is closed.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_grasper_holding_object
atomic_propositions.description: Before the action, the active grasper is holding some object.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_move_has_x
atomic_propositions.description: Before move_grasper, the action arguments include a numeric x.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_move_has_y
atomic_propositions.description: Before move_grasper, the action arguments include a numeric y.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_move_x_in_bounds
atomic_propositions.description: Before move_grasper, the provided x is within bounds.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_move_y_in_bounds
atomic_propositions.description: Before move_grasper, the provided y is within bounds.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_obj_id_is_integer
atomic_propositions.description: Before highlight/unhighlight, obj_id is an integer at the env layer.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_grasper_resting_on_graspable
atomic_propositions.description: Before close_grasper, the lowered grasper is resting on a graspable object.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_held_object_resting_on_support
atomic_propositions.description: Before open_grasper while holding, the held object's resting_on tag is not null.

atomic_propositions.name: pre_support_can_hold_object
atomic_propositions.description: Before open_grasper while holding, the supporting object can support the held object.

atomic_propositions.name: post_grasper_lowered
atomic_propositions.description: After the action, the active grasper is lowered.

atomic_propositions.name: post_grasper_closed
atomic_propositions.description: After the action, the active grasper is closed.

atomic_propositions.name: post_grasper_holding_object
atomic_propositions.description: After the action, the active grasper is holding some object.

atomic_propositions.name: post_held_object_xy_matches_grasper_xy
atomic_propositions.description: After a move while holding, the held object's x,y matches the grasper's x,y.

atomic_propositions.name: post_grasper_clears_scene
atomic_propositions.description: After raise_grasper, the grasper is high enough to clear the scene.

atomic_propositions.name: post_grasper_resting_on_is_null
atomic_propositions.description: After the action, the grasper's resting_on tag is null.

atomic_propositions.name: post_held_object_resting_on_is_null
atomic_propositions.description: After the action, the held object's resting_on tag is null.

atomic_propositions.name: post_lowered_onto_highest_support_or_ground
atomic_propositions.description: After lower_grasper, the grasper or held object is lowered onto the highest support below or the ground.

rules.natural_language: At the env layer, move_grasper is only legal when both x and y are numeric.
At the env layer, highlight and unhighlight require an integer obj_id.
Any grasper primitive requires an existing grasper before execution.
If an explicit grasper id is used, it must refer to a real grasper.
notes: This rule uses an AP that the verifier may synthesize only when an explicit grasper id exists.

rules.natural_language: Highlighting actions require the referenced object to exist before execution.
move_grasper is only legal when the grasper is raised beforehand.
If move_grasper uses x, that x must already satisfy the allowed bounds.
If move_grasper uses y, that y must already satisfy the allowed bounds.
lower_grasper is only legal when the grasper is raised beforehand.
raise_grasper is only legal when the grasper is lowered beforehand.
close_grasper is only legal when the grasper is open beforehand.
open_grasper is only legal when the grasper is closed beforehand.
If open_grasper is executed while holding an object, the grasper must already be lowered.
If open_grasper is executed while holding an object, the held object must already be resting on some support.
If open_grasper is executed while holding an object, the support below must be able to support the held object.
After close_grasper, the grasper is closed.
If close_grasper is executed while lowered on a graspable support, the grasper holds an object afterward.
After a valid open_grasper, the grasper is open; if it was holding an object, it no longer is.
notes: This nested implication is an AST-level shorthand over the same static transition snapshot.

rules.natural_language: After lower_grasper, the grasper is lowered.
After raise_grasper, the grasper is no longer lowered.
After raise_grasper, the grasper resting_on tag is null; if holding an object, the held object's resting_on tag is also null.
After raise_grasper, the grasper clears the scene.
After move_grasper while holding an object, the held object's x,y matches the grasper's x,y.
After lower_grasper, the grasper or held object is lowered onto the highest support below or ground.

benefits: Primitive actions now match the simulator exactly.
APs are static facts about one transition snapshot.
Rules are easy to combine into higher-level specifications.
remaining_abstractions: Support geometry is represented by derived APs such as pre_support_can_hold_object.
Scene-clearance and exact lowering target remain abstract APs computed by the verifier.
recommended_next_step: Build a Python state-to-AP extractor for these names.
Then evaluate the AST formulas over predicted or real transition snapshots.
